title: cytisus supinus subsp. pannonicus
intro: Cytisus supinus subsp. pannonicus, also known as Hungarian laburnum or Pannonian kidney vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic qualities, which have been utilized to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with Cytisus supinus subsp. pannonicus include relief from digestive problems, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
